name: bsd-socker
summary: Author's Note (2020.2.9) ↝ I do not plan to pick this project back up again. A significant amount of its functionality – such as sniffing packets containing payloads in a variety of formats and determining many details about them – is complete. Other features, such as injection, are not – but should be easy to figure out based on the existing code. Hopefully it can serve as a starting-point or an educational example for others.

documentation.readme: Author's Note (2020.2.9) ↝ I do not plan to pick this project back up again. A significant amount of its functionality – such as sniffing packets containing payloads in a variety of formats and determining many details about them – is complete. Other features, such as injection, are not – but should be easy to figure out based on the existing code. Hopefully it can serve as a starting-point or an educational example for others.
usage_sections: Compiles with any C compiler on any BSD-based system
Works on any BSD-based system that provides the BPF device (try man bpf)
Is written and architected in an extremely clean, readable, and thus

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_wireless_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Wireless category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_wireless_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Wireless system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for bsd-socker (lukehollenback/bsd-socker). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for bsd-socker. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.
